text: Urban fires always constitute one of the major threats for smart cities, because they cause immense financial losses and human casualties for cities, and the existing fire detection systems have a huge number of disadvantages like slow response time, or even imprecise location of the fire. In order to solve these complex problems, this paper put forward an advanced Smart Fire Detection System (SFDS), which is based on an improved YOLOv8 model. The improved model can use the strength of deep learning models to quickly and accurately discover the features of fire, and avoid false alarms. Moreover, the whole SFDS is divided into IoT layer, Fog layer, Cloud layer and Application layer according to the hierarchical architecture of the edge computing paradigm. Based on the characteristics of the four layers, not only the processing functions are distributed, but the edges process the data in real time, and the locations of fires are given in seconds. The experiment shows that the improved model has excellent detection performance, the precision of the eight classes of objects is as high as 97.1%. The proposed system has great potential for real-time detection of other disaster situations in smart cities, such as flood disaster and urban gas leak disasters, which can also be effectively applied to public safety management, forest monitoring, and so on, so much. Therefore, it provides strong support for the construction of smart security systems in the future.
